summary: This bundle refreshes deterministic proof that `doctor status --json` emits a read-only `cautilus.agent_status.v1` orientation packet covering binary health, agent-surface readiness, adapter state, claim-state availability, scan scope, and branch choices.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs/guides/cli.md claim that `doctor status --json` emits `cautilus.agent_status.v1` as a read-only orientation packet over the listed readiness and branch-choice fields is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: The agent must follow a particular branch after reading the packet
Evaluation, review, improvement, edits, or commits are launched by doctor status
Every possible adapter or claim-state shape is covered
The packet is a global product verdict

notableAssertions: binary health, agent surface readiness, adapter state, claim state, runner readiness, and scan scope are present
branch text preserves the claim scan command and review-budget boundary
orientation output does not offer evaluation, review variant execution, improvement, or git commit work

commandEvidence.command: ./bin/cautilus doctor status --repo-root . --json | jq '{schemaVersion, mode, status, binary: .binary.status, agentSurface: .agentSurface.status, adapterValid: .adapter.valid, claimState: .claimState.status, scanScope: .scanScope, branchCount: (.nextBranches | length)}'

decision.reason: The focused CLI test proves the no-input orientation packet shape and non-launch boundary, and a current command run confirms `doctor status --json` emits `cautilus.agent_status.v1` with binary, agent-surface, adapter, claim-state, scan-scope, and branch-choice fields.
